heText: בְּרֵאשִׁית בָּרָא אֱלֹהִים אֵת הַשָּׁמַיִם וְאֵת הָאָֽרֶץ׃
enText: At the very beginning of everything, God crafted the heavens and the earth. The earth was an empty void, shapeless, and darkness covered the deep waters. The Spirit of God hovered above the surface of the waters. God declared, "Let there be light!" And light came into being. God observed the light, that it was good, and He separated the light from the darkness. He named the light 'Day' and the darkness 'Night'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the first day. Then God spoke again, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, separating water from water!" God formed this expanse, dividing the waters below it from the waters above it. He named the expanse 'Sky'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the second day. God commanded, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered into one place, and let the dry land appear!" And it happened. God named the dry land 'Land' and the gathered waters 'Seas'. God saw that it was good. Then God spoke, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seeds and trees yielding fruit with their seeds, each according to its kind!" And it happened. The land brought forth grass, seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees producing fruit with their seeds, each of its kind. God saw that it was good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the third day. God then stated, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to distinguish day from night, and let them serve as signs for seasons, for days, and for years! Let them be sources of light in the sky's expanse to illuminate the earth!" God fashioned two grand lights: the larger light to govern the day and the smaller light to govern the night, along with the stars. God placed them in the expanse of the sky to cast light upon the earth. So there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day. God commanded, "Let the waters teem with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky!" God then created the great sea monsters and every living moving creature that the waters brought forth, according to its kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth!" So there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day. God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth, which has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food." And it came to be. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

heText: וַיַּרְא אֱלֹהִים אֶת־הָאוֹר כִּי־טוֹב וַיַּבְדֵּל אֱלֹהִים בֵּין הָאוֹר וּבֵין הַחֹֽשֶׁךְ׃
enText: At the very beginning of everything, God crafted the heavens and the earth. The earth was an empty void, shapeless, and darkness covered the deep waters. The Spirit of God hovered above the surface of the waters. God declared, "Let there be light!" And light came into being. God observed the light, that it was good, and He separated the light from the darkness. He named the light 'Day' and the darkness 'Night'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the first day. Then God spoke again, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, separating water from water!" God formed this expanse, dividing the waters below it from the waters above it. He named the expanse 'Sky'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the second day. God commanded, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered into one place, and let the dry land appear!" And it happened. God named the dry land 'Land' and the gathered waters 'Seas'. God saw that it was good. Then God spoke, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seeds and trees yielding fruit with their seeds, each according to its kind!" And it happened. The land brought forth grass, seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees producing fruit with their seeds, each of its kind. God saw that it was good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the third day. God then stated, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to distinguish day from night, and let them serve as signs for seasons, for days, and for years! Let them be sources of light in the sky's expanse to illuminate the earth!" God fashioned two grand lights: the larger light to govern the day and the smaller light to govern the night, along with the stars. God placed them in the expanse of the sky to cast light upon the earth. So there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day. God commanded, "Let the waters teem with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky!" God then created the great sea monsters and every living moving creature that the waters brought forth, according to its kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth!" So there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day. God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth, which has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food." And it came to be. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

heText: וַיֹּאמֶר אֱלֹהִים יִקָּווּ הַמַּיִם מִתַּחַת הַשָּׁמַיִם אֶל־מָקוֹם אֶחָד וְתֵרָאֶה הַיַּבָּשָׁה וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
enText: At the very beginning of everything, God crafted the heavens and the earth. The earth was an empty void, shapeless, and darkness covered the deep waters. The Spirit of God hovered above the surface of the waters. God declared, "Let there be light!" And light came into being. God observed the light, that it was good, and He separated the light from the darkness. He named the light 'Day' and the darkness 'Night'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the first day. Then God spoke again, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, separating water from water!" God formed this expanse, dividing the waters below it from the waters above it. He named the expanse 'Sky'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the second day. God commanded, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered into one place, and let the dry land appear!" And it happened. God named the dry land 'Land' and the gathered waters 'Seas'. God saw that it was good. Then God spoke, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seeds and trees yielding fruit with their seeds, each according to its kind!" And it happened. The land brought forth grass, seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees producing fruit with their seeds, each of its kind. God saw that it was good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the third day. God then stated, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to distinguish day from night, and let them serve as signs for seasons, for days, and for years! Let them be sources of light in the sky's expanse to illuminate the earth!" God fashioned two grand lights: the larger light to govern the day and the smaller light to govern the night, along with the stars. God placed them in the expanse of the sky to cast light upon the earth. So there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day. God commanded, "Let the waters teem with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky!" God then created the great sea monsters and every living moving creature that the waters brought forth, according to its kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth!" So there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day. God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth, which has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food." And it came to be. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

God fashioned two grand lights: the larger light to govern the day and the smaller light to govern the night, along with the stars. God placed them in the expanse of the sky to cast light upon the earth. So there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day. God commanded, "Let the waters teem with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky!" God then created the great sea monsters and every living moving creature that the waters brought forth, according to its kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth!" So there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day. God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth, which has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food." And it came to be. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

heText: וַיַּעַשׂ אֱלֹהִים אֶת־שְׁנֵי הַמְּאֹרֹת הַגְּדֹלִים אֶת־הַמָּאוֹר הַגָּדֹל לְמֶמְשֶׁלֶת הַיּוֹם וְאֶת־הַמָּאוֹר הַקָּטֹן לְמֶמְשֶׁלֶת הַלַּיְלָה וְאֵת הַכּוֹכָבִֽים׃
enText: At the very beginning of everything, God crafted the heavens and the earth. The earth was an empty void, shapeless, and darkness covered the deep waters. The Spirit of God hovered above the surface of the waters. God declared, "Let there be light!" And light came into being. God observed the light, that it was good, and He separated the light from the darkness. He named the light 'Day' and the darkness 'Night'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the first day. Then God spoke again, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, separating water from water!" God formed this expanse, dividing the waters below it from the waters above it. He named the expanse 'Sky'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the second day. God commanded, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered into one place, and let the dry land appear!" And it happened. God named the dry land 'Land' and the gathered waters 'Seas'. God saw that it was good. Then God spoke, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seeds and trees yielding fruit with their seeds, each according to its kind!" And it happened. The land brought forth grass, seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees producing fruit with their seeds, each of its kind. God saw that it was good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the third day. God then stated, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to distinguish day from night, and let them serve as signs for seasons, for days, and for years! Let them be sources of light in the sky's expanse to illuminate the earth!" God fashioned two grand lights: the larger light to govern the day and the smaller light to govern the night, along with the stars. God placed them in the expanse of the sky to cast light upon the earth. So there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day. God commanded, "Let the waters teem with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky!" God then created the great sea monsters and every living moving creature that the waters brought forth, according to its kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth!" So there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day. God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

enText: At the very beginning of everything, God crafted the heavens and the earth. The earth was an empty void, shapeless, and darkness covered the deep waters. The Spirit of God hovered above the surface of the waters. God declared, "Let there be light!" And light came into being. God observed the light, that it was good, and He separated the light from the darkness. He named the light 'Day' and the darkness 'Night'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the first day. Then God spoke again, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, separating water from water!" God formed this expanse, dividing the waters below it from the waters above it. He named the expanse 'Sky'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the second day. God commanded, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered into one place, and let the dry land appear!" And it happened. God named the dry land 'Land' and the gathered waters 'Seas'. God saw that it was good. Then God spoke, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seeds and trees yielding fruit with their seeds, each according to its kind!" And it happened. The land brought forth grass, seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees producing fruit with their seeds, each of its kind. God saw that it was good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the third day. God then stated, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to distinguish day from night, and let them serve as signs for seasons, for days, and for years! Let them be sources of light in the sky's expanse to illuminate the earth!" God fashioned two grand lights: the larger light to govern the day and the smaller light to govern the night, along with the stars. God placed them in the expanse of the sky to cast light upon the earth. So there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day. God commanded, "Let the waters teem with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky!" God then created the great sea monsters and every living moving creature that the waters brought forth, according to its kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth!" So there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day. God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth, which has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food." And it came to be. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

heText: וַיֹּאמֶר אֱלֹהִים נַֽעֲשֶׂה אָדָם בְּצַלְמֵנוּ כִּדְמוּתֵנוּ וְיִרְדּוּ בִדְגַת הַיָּם וּבְעוֹף הַשָּׁמַיִם וּבַבְּהֵמָה וּבְכׇל־הָאָרֶץ וּבְכׇל־הָרֶמֶשׂ הָֽרֹמֵשׂ עַל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃

Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" God further stated, "Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you. And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth, which has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food." And it came to be. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

heText: וּֽלְכׇל־חַיַּת הָאָרֶץ וּלְכׇל־עוֹף הַשָּׁמַיִם וּלְכֹל׀ רוֹמֵשׂ עַל־הָאָרֶץ אֲשֶׁר־בּוֹ נֶפֶשׁ חַיָּה אֶת־כׇּל־יֶרֶק עֵשֶׂב לְאׇכְלָה וַֽיְהִי־כֵֽן׃
enText: At the very beginning of everything, God crafted the heavens and the earth. The earth was an empty void, shapeless, and darkness covered the deep waters. The Spirit of God hovered above the surface of the waters. God declared, "Let there be light!" And light came into being. God observed the light, that it was good, and He separated the light from the darkness. He named the light 'Day' and the darkness 'Night'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the first day. Then God spoke again, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, separating water from water!" God formed this expanse, dividing the waters below it from the waters above it. He named the expanse 'Sky'. So there was evening, and there was morning—the second day. God commanded, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered into one place, and let the dry land appear!" And it happened. God named the dry land 'Land' and the gathered waters 'Seas'. God saw that it was good. Then God spoke, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seeds and trees yielding fruit with their seeds, each according to its kind!" And it happened. The land brought forth grass, seed-bearing plants of every kind, and trees producing fruit with their seeds, each of its kind. God saw that it was good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the third day. God then stated, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to distinguish day from night, and let them serve as signs for seasons, for days, and for years! Let them be sources of light in the sky's expanse to illuminate the earth!" God fashioned two grand lights: the larger light to govern the day and the smaller light to govern the night, along with the stars. God placed them in the expanse of the sky to cast light upon the earth. So there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day. God commanded, "Let the waters teem with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky!" God then created the great sea monsters and every living moving creature that the waters brought forth, according to its kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good. God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth!" So there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day. God then commanded, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild beasts of the earth, each according to its kind!" And it came to be. God made the wild beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. God saw that it was good. Then God declared, "Let us create humankind in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creature that creeps on the earth!" So God created humankind in His own image, in the image of God He created them; male and female He created them. God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it! Rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth!" And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth, which has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food." And it came to be. God surveyed everything He had made, and indeed, it was exceedingly good. So there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.
